title: The Master Asleep in the Boat
subtitle: Christ sleeps in the stern while the storm rages, then rises and commands the wind and the sea

stillness: Be still, and let your heart grow quiet.
He says in the Song: I sleep, and my heart keeps watch.
Let your soul keep watch in Him while all else sleeps.
gospel: When evening came, the Lord Jesus dismissed the crowds.
He went up into a little boat to cross the lake of Genesareth.
And His disciples followed Him, crossing over with Him.
And behold, a great commotion arose upon the sea,
so that the boat was nearly covered by the surging waves.
But He was sleeping in the stern, upon a wooden pillow.
And they came running to Him and woke Him, saying:
Lord, save us; for we are perishing.
And He said to them: Why are you afraid, you of little faith?
Then rising up, He commanded the winds and the sea:
And the storm ceased, and a great calm was made,
so that not a trace of any disturbance remained.
behold: Attend to each thing as though you were present.
Behold the Lord, who governs the whole world by His divine power,
boarding a small boat, that He might sail in it.
Behold Him who guards His people with an eternal watchfulness,
Behold Him sleeping in the stern, upon a wooden pillow,
showing, as Chrysostom says, His humility.
And behold the great commotion upon the sea,
the boat nearly buried beneath the inundating waves.
See the disciples, set in peril, running back to Him,
and waking Him: Lord, save us, for we perish.
consider: He slept in body, but in His divinity He kept watch.
He was sleeping, who much of the night kept vigil in prayer,
and much of the day labored in preaching.
He willed to sleep, that He might show the truth of His human nature;
and He willed to sleep, that He might test the faith of His disciples,
not because He did not know their hearts, but that they might know themselves.
O truthful disciples! says Origen.
You have the Savior with you, and you fear danger;
Life is with you, and you are anxious about death!
That they said Save us was of trust;
that they said We perish was of faint-heartedness;
that they woke Him was of little faith.
Why are you afraid, you of little faith?
As if to say: if you had faith, you would not fear,
but would calm the very winds and the sea.
For as gold is tested in fire, so faith in trials.
Of little faith is the one who, set in adversity,
in hunger, in pressure, and the like, murmurs, fears, and bears it impatiently.
Therefore faith is most necessary in dangers:
this is the victory that conquers the world, our faith.
He commanded the winds and the sea as the Lord of creation.
As man He sleeps in the boat; as God He commands the wind and the sea.
The sleep showed the man; the calm showed the God.
The senseless winds and waters obey Him, as the creature its Creator;
and in this the rational creatures are rebuked,
who do not obey their Creator, while the senseless creatures obey Him.
And in that, when wakened and asked, He set them free,
He shows that He always wills to be asked by us.
Often He exposes us to pressures, because He wills that we pray to Him,
and so be delivered from them.
Therefore the just are pressed, that being pressed they may cry out;
crying out, they may be heard; and being heard, may glorify God.
prayer: command the blasts and motions of suggestions and temptations;
come, and walk upon the waves of my heart,
that all within me may become serene and tranquil.
Let my heart rest in You, my God;
let my heart, a great sea swelling with waves,
sleep to all things that are beneath heaven,
that I may embrace You, my one good,
and contemplate You, the light of my eyes,
and rejoicing may sing and say:
I sleep, and my heart keeps watch;
and that other word: In peace, in the selfsame, I will sleep and take my rest.

latinJewel.latin: Quasi enim homo navem ascendit, sed quasi Deus mare conturbat; quasi homo in navi dormit, sed quasi Deus ventis et mari imperat, et eorum furorem verbo coercet.
latinJewel.english: For as man He boards the boat, but as God He troubles the sea; as man He sleeps in the boat, but as God He commands the winds and the sea, and restrains their fury by a word.
